name: RiskySPN
summary:
RiskySPNs
RiskySPNs is a collection of PowerShell scripts focused on detecting and abusing accounts associated with SPNs (Service Principal Name). This module can assist blue teams to identify potentially risky SPNs as well as red teams to escalate privileges by leveraging Kerberos and Active Directory.

documentation.readme: RiskySPNs RiskySPNs is a collection of PowerShell scripts focused on detecting and abusing accounts associated with SPNs (Service Principal Name). This module can assist blue teams to identify potentially risky SPNs as well as red teams to escalate privileges by leveraging Kerberos and Active Directory.

arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: RiskySPNs RiskySPNs is a collection of PowerShell scripts focused on detecting and abusing accounts associated with SPNs (Service Principal Name). This module can assist blue teams to identify potentially risky SPNs as well as red teams to escalate privileges by leveraging Kerberos and Active Directory.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Import-Module .\RiskySPNs.psm1`. Further invocations from upstream: `Get-Help Get-TGSCipher -Full`; `Get-TGSCipher -SPN "MSSQLSvc/prodDB.company.com:1433"`. trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
